title: Fix Settings authentication, add 'Other' gender option, and surface change password UI

requirements.summary: Fix Settings page authentication to properly recognize session-authenticated users and prevent false 'not authenticated' errors
acceptanceCriteria: A user who has logged in via the Login page can open Settings and see all settings fields without any authentication error message.
Unauthenticated users still see the login prompt on the Settings page.
Changing display name, visibility, language, or gender saves successfully for an authenticated session user.

file_operations.description: Fix the authentication check to use useSessionAuth instead of Internet Identity. Replace the current auth logic with const { username, displayName } = useCurrentUser(); and const isAuthenticated = !!username;. Remove any Internet Identity checks and ensure all settings controls are available when a session token exists. Keep the AccountsDisabledNotice for truly unauthenticated users only.

requirements.summary: Add 'Other' option to the gender selection field on the Settings page
acceptanceCriteria: The gender dropdown/selector on the Settings page includes 'Other' as a selectable option.
Selecting 'Other' and saving persists the value correctly.
The backend UserProfile or UserSettings type accepts and stores 'Other' as a valid gender value.

file_operations.description: Add 'Other' as a selectable option in the gender field dropdown/selector. The backend Gender enum already includes 'other' as a variant (per backend-interface), so ensure the frontend UI includes it alongside Male and Female options. Use the value 'other' (lowercase) to match the backend enum.

requirements.summary: Add a 'Change Password' section to the Settings page with current password, new password, and confirmation fields
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Change Password' section is visible on the Settings page for authenticated users.
The form has three fields: Current Password, New Password, Confirm New Password.
Submitting with a matching new/confirm password and correct current password updates the password and shows a success toast.
Submitting with an incorrect current password shows an error toast.
Submitting with mismatched new/confirm passwords shows a validation error without calling the update function.

file_operations.description: Add a changePassword function to the useSessionAuth hook that accepts currentPassword, newPassword, and newPasswordConfirm parameters. Validate that newPassword matches newPasswordConfirm locally. Verify currentPassword against the stored user password in localStorage. If valid, update the stored password for the current user. Return success/error status to allow the UI to show appropriate toast messages.

Add a 'Change Password' section to the Settings page layout (visible only when authenticated). Include three password input fields: Current Password, New Password, and Confirm New Password. On form submit, validate that New Password and Confirm New Password match (show validation error if not). Call the changePassword function from useSessionAuth with the three values. Display a success toast on successful password change or an error toast if the current password is incorrect or update fails.
